subject: English
read_aloud: Can you spell PLANET? It has 6 letters. P-L-A-N-E-T.
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! P-L-A-N-E-T! You spelled PLANET perfectly, space explorer!
incorrect_response: Nearly! Let's break it down: PL-AN-ET. P-L-A-N-E-T. Try sounding out each part!
hint: Break it into parts: PLAN-ET. You know how to spell PLAN, then just add ET!

subject: English
read_aloud: What does ORBIT mean? When something goes around something else!
correct_answers: when something goes around something else
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! An ORBIT is the path something takes going around something else! Like Earth orbiting the Sun!
incorrect_response: An ORBIT is when something travels around something else. The Earth ORBITS the Sun, and the Moon ORBITS the Earth!
hint: Think about what the Earth does around the Sun. It goes around and around in a circle...

subject: English
read_aloud: How do you spell HAMMER? It's a tool you use in woodworking!
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! H-A-M-M-E-R! You nailed that spelling!
incorrect_response: Let's sound it out: HAM-MER. H-A-M-M-E-R. Notice the double M in the middle!
hint: It starts with HAM (like the food!) then MER. Watch out for the double letter in the middle!
mnemonic: HAMMER has MM in the Middle - just like two nails side by side!

subject: English
read_aloud: What's a CONSTELLATION? A pattern of stars in the sky!
correct_answers: pattern of stars in the sky
group of stars that make a shape
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! A CONSTELLATION is a pattern of stars in the sky! Like Orion the Hunter!
incorrect_response: A CONSTELLATION is a group of stars that form a pattern or shape in the sky. People have been spotting them for thousands of years!
hint: Look up at the night sky. When you connect the dots between stars, you can see shapes and patterns. What are those called?
metacognitive_prompt: Can you name any constellations you've spotted?

subject: English
read_aloud: Spell GALAXY. G-A-L-A-X-Y. A galaxy is a huge collection of stars!
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! G-A-L-A-X-Y! You spelled GALAXY like a true astronomer!
incorrect_response: Let's break it up: GAL-AX-Y. G-A-L-A-X-Y. Notice it has two A's!
hint: It starts with GAL, then AX, then Y. G-A-L...

subject: English
read_aloud: What does GRAVITY mean? It's the force that keeps your feet on the ground!
correct_answers: what keeps us on the ground
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! GRAVITY is the force that pulls things towards each other! It keeps us on the ground and the Moon orbiting Earth!
incorrect_response: GRAVITY is the force that pulls things together. It's what makes things fall down and keeps us from floating away!
hint: When you drop something, it falls DOWN. What invisible force is pulling it?
metacognitive_prompt: What would happen without gravity? Think about astronauts on the space station!

subject: English
read_aloud: Can you spell MEASURE? You need to measure when woodworking!
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! M-E-A-S-U-R-E! Measure twice, cut once!
incorrect_response: This is a tricky one! MEA-SURE. M-E-A-S-U-R-E. The EA makes an 'eh' sound here!
hint: Break it into two parts: MEAS-URE. Start with M-E-A-S...
metacognitive_prompt: What tricky part did you find hardest? The EA or the URE?

subject: English
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! R-O-C-K-E-T! Your spelling just blasted off!
incorrect_response: Let's sound it out: ROCK-ET. R-O-C-K-E-T. You know ROCK, just add ET!
hint: It starts with ROCK (like a stone!) then add ET at the end.

subject: English
read_aloud: What does EXPERIMENT mean? Scientists do them to find things out!
correct_answers: a test to find something out
testing something to learn about it
trying something to see what happens
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! An EXPERIMENT is a test you do to find something out or prove an idea! You're a real scientist!
incorrect_response: An EXPERIMENT is a test where you try something to discover what happens. Scientists use experiments to learn about the world!
hint: When scientists want to find out if something is true, they set up a test. What's that test called?
metacognitive_prompt: Can you think of an experiment you'd like to try?

subject: English
read_aloud: What sound does the PH make in the word PHOSPHORUS? It's a glowing element!
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! PH makes the F sound! So PHOSPHORUS starts with an F sound even though it's spelled with PH!
incorrect_response: PH makes the F sound! So PHOSPHORUS sounds like it starts with F. Other examples: PHONE, PHOTO!
hint: Think of the word PHONE. What sound does PH make there?
metacognitive_prompt: Can you think of other words where PH sounds like F?

subject: English
read_aloud: Can you spell RHYTHM? It's important in violin music! R-H-Y-T-H-M.
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! R-H-Y-T-H-M! That's a really tricky word and you got it! Maestro!
incorrect_response: RHYTHM is one of the trickiest words! R-H-Y-T-H-M. It has no normal vowels, just the letter Y!
hint: This word has no A, E, I, O, or U! Y does the vowel job. R-H-Y-T-H-M.
mnemonic: Rhythm Has Your Two Hips Moving!
metacognitive_prompt: What's unusual about this word compared to most English words?

subject: English
read_aloud: What does ATMOSPHERE mean? Every planet can have one!
correct_answers: layer of gases around a planet
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! The ATMOSPHERE is the layer of gases surrounding a planet! Earth's atmosphere is what we breathe!
incorrect_response: The ATMOSPHERE is the blanket of gases around a planet. Earth's atmosphere has the oxygen we breathe and protects us from space!
hint: Think about what surrounds the Earth like an invisible blanket. It's the air and gases all around our planet.

subject: English
read_aloud: In the word WRENCH (a woodworking tool), the W is silent. What sound does WRENCH start with?
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! WRENCH starts with the R sound even though there's a silent W! Sneaky English!
incorrect_response: The W in WRENCH is silent! So it starts with the R sound: R-ENCH. Just like WRITE, WRONG, and WRAP!
hint: Say WRENCH out loud. You don't hear the W, do you? What sound do you actually hear first?
metacognitive_prompt: Can you think of other words with a silent W at the start?

subject: English
read_aloud: Can you spell SATELLITE? S-A-T-E-L-L-I-T-E. It orbits a planet!
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! S-A-T-E-L-L-I-T-E! That's a challenging word and you spelled it! Out of this world!
incorrect_response: Let's break it down: SAT-EL-LITE. S-A-T-E-L-L-I-T-E. Watch for the double L in the middle!
hint: Start with SAT, then EL, then LITE. S-A-T-E-L-L-I-T-E. The tricky part is the double L!
metacognitive_prompt: Which part of the word was trickiest to remember?

subject: English
read_aloud: The word SCIENCE has a tricky start. What two letters make the S sound at the beginning? SC-ience!
correct_response: Brilliant Reuben! SC makes the S sound in SCIENCE! The C is almost hiding behind the S!
incorrect_response: In SCIENCE, the letters S and C team up to make just an S sound. SC-IENCE. It's one of those tricky English spellings!
hint: Look at how SCIENCE is spelled: S-C-I-E-N-C-E. The first two letters are S and...
metacognitive_prompt: Can you think of other words where SC makes an S sound? Like SCISSORS!
